name: Pacific Islands Forum 1971 + 2005 Agreement Establishing PIF
summary: The Pacific Islands Forum (PIF) is a regional supranational meta-framework foundational-Pacific-Islands-Forum framework adopted by the 7 founding Pacific states in Wellington — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Pacific regional supranational framework operating on formal Pacific-Ocean framework + only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Australia-New-Zealand-Pacific-Islands developed-developing mixed-membership framework (PIF founded 5 August 1971 in Wellington New Zealand as South Pacific Forum + renamed Pacific Islands Forum 2000 + 2005 Agreement Establishing the Pacific Islands Forum signed 27 October 2005 in Madang Papua New Guinea + effective 18 June 2008 granting PIF formal international legal-personality framework — formal foundational PIF framework establishing comprehensive PIF organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-regional-treaty frameworks which operate on continental-mass frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Pacific regional supranational framework operating formal Pacific-Ocean framework + only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Australia-New-Zealand-Pacific-Islands developed-developing mixed-membership framework + only modern regional supranational framework granting formal Republics-monarchies-British-Crown-realms-mixed-membership framework + 165-million-square-kilometres geographic coverage framework spanning 30% Earth's surface), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Boe Declaration on Regional Security 2018 framework (Boe Declaration on Regional Security adopted 5 September 2018 at 49th Pacific Islands Forum Nauru providing formal Pacific-regional climate-change-as-single-greatest-threat framework — formal foundational Pacific-regional security framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational framework operating formal climate-change-as-single-greatest-threat framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent framework (2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent adopted 14 July 2022 at 51st Pacific Islands Forum Suva Fiji providing formal Pacific-regional Blue-Pacific-Continent framework — formal foundational Pacific-regional 2050-Strategy framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Blue-Pacific-Continent framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Pacific Islands Forum Kiribati Withdrawal Reinstatement framework (Micronesian Kiribati + Marshall Islands + FSM + Nauru + Palau announced PIF withdrawal February 2021 over Secretary-General selection dispute + Suva Agreement signed June 2022 reinstating Micronesian states — formal foundational PIF reinstatement framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Micronesian-quintet-withdrawal-reinstatement framework). The Pacific Islands Forum is operating upon 18 PIF member states + 14 PIF dialogue partners as of 2026 (Australia + Cook Islands + Federated States of Micronesia + Fiji + French Polynesia + Kiribati + Marshall Islands + Nauru + New Caledonia + New Zealand + Niue + Palau + Papua New Guinea + Samoa + Solomon Islands + Tonga + Tuvalu + Vanuatu) — comprehensive Pacific Ocean regional coverage framework. The Forum is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Pacific Islands Forum 2050 Strategy + Boe Declaration frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Pacific-regional cross-border-children's-rights framework + Pacific-regional climate-displacement-children's-rights framework. The Pacific Islands Forum Leaders' Meeting + Pacific Islands Forum Secretariat + Pacific Islands Forum Secretary-General are the formal PIF bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies (Judicial Committee of the Privy Council JCPC as final court of appeal for Cook Islands + Kiribati + Tuvalu + others). The Pacific Islands Forum is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: PIF Founding
statutory_framework.relevance: Pacific Islands Forum founded 5 August 1971 in Wellington New Zealand as South Pacific Forum + renamed Pacific Islands Forum 2000 — formal foundational PIF framework.

statutory_framework.citation: 2005 Agreement Establishing the Pacific Islands Forum
statutory_framework.title: Agreement Establishing PIF
statutory_framework.relevance: Agreement Establishing the Pacific Islands Forum signed 27 October 2005 in Madang Papua New Guinea + effective 18 June 2008 — formal foundational PIF treaty framework granting PIF formal international legal-personality framework.

statutory_framework.title: Biketawa Declaration
statutory_framework.relevance: Biketawa Declaration adopted 28 October 2000 at 31st Pacific Islands Forum Kiribati providing formal PIF crisis-response framework — formal foundational PIF crisis-response framework.

statutory_framework.title: Boe Declaration
statutory_framework.relevance: Boe Declaration on Regional Security adopted 5 September 2018 at 49th Pacific Islands Forum Nauru — formal foundational Pacific-regional climate-change-as-single-greatest-threat framework.

statutory_framework.citation: 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent 2022
statutory_framework.title: 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent
statutory_framework.relevance: 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent adopted 14 July 2022 at 51st Pacific Islands Forum Suva Fiji — formal foundational Pacific-regional 2050-Strategy framework.

apex_courts.name: Pacific Islands Forum Leaders' Meeting
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for PIF.

apex_courts.name: Pacific Islands Forum Secretary-General
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative head body for PIF.

anonymisation_convention: PIF-related communications operate via PIF Leaders' Meeting framework; individual communications not formally accepted under PIF framework.

key_developments.title: South Pacific Forum founded
key_developments.description: South Pacific Forum founded 5 August 1971 in Wellington New Zealand by Australia + Cook Islands + Fiji + Nauru + New Zealand + Tonga + Western Samoa — formal foundational PIF framework operational.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Rarotonga Pacific Nuclear-Free Zone
key_developments.description: South Pacific Nuclear Free Zone Treaty Treaty of Rarotonga signed 6 August 1985 in Rarotonga Cook Islands + effective 11 December 1986 — formal foundational Pacific-regional nuclear-free-zone framework.

key_developments.title: Wellington Convention on driftnet fishing
key_developments.description: Convention for the Prohibition of Fishing with Long Driftnets in the South Pacific Wellington Convention signed 24 November 1989 — formal foundational Pacific-regional fishing-protection framework.

key_developments.title: Biketawa Declaration + South Pacific Forum renamed Pacific Islands Forum
key_developments.description: Biketawa Declaration adopted 28 October 2000 at 31st Pacific Islands Forum Kiribati + South Pacific Forum renamed Pacific Islands Forum October 2000 — formal foundational PIF crisis-response + PIF re-naming framework.

key_developments.title: Agreement Establishing PIF
key_developments.description: Agreement Establishing the Pacific Islands Forum signed 27 October 2005 in Madang Papua New Guinea — formal foundational PIF treaty framework granting PIF formal international legal-personality framework.

key_developments.title: PIF Agreement enters force
key_developments.description: Agreement Establishing the Pacific Islands Forum entered force 18 June 2008 — formal foundational PIF treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: Boe Declaration
key_developments.description: Boe Declaration on Regional Security adopted 5 September 2018 at 49th Pacific Islands Forum Nauru — formal foundational Pacific-regional climate-change-as-single-greatest-threat framework.

key_developments.title: Micronesian quintet withdrawal announced
key_developments.description: Micronesian Kiribati + Marshall Islands + FSM + Nauru + Palau announced PIF withdrawal February 2021 over Secretary-General selection dispute — formal PIF Micronesian-quintet-withdrawal framework.

key_developments.title: Suva Agreement reinstating Micronesian quintet + 2050 Strategy
key_developments.description: Suva Agreement signed June 2022 reinstating Micronesian quintet + 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent adopted 14 July 2022 at 51st Pacific Islands Forum Suva Fiji — formal PIF reinstatement + 2050 Strategy framework.

key_developments.title: Pacific Policing Initiative
key_developments.description: Pacific Policing Initiative adopted 30 August 2024 at 53rd Pacific Islands Forum Nuku'alofa Tonga — formal foundational Pacific-regional policing framework.

structural_findings: Pacific Islands Forum operates a regional supranational framework — places PIF in the regional supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern Pacific regional supranational framework operating on formal Pacific-Ocean framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Australia-New-Zealand-Pacific-Islands developed-developing mixed-membership framework + only modern regional supranational framework granting formal Republics-monarchies-British-Crown-realms-mixed-membership framework + 165-million-square-kilometres geographic coverage framework spanning 30% Earth's surface.

Central jurisdiction of formal Boe Declaration on Regional Security 2018 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational framework operating formal climate-change-as-single-greatest-threat framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Blue-Pacific-Continent framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Pacific Islands Forum Kiribati Withdrawal Reinstatement framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Micronesian-quintet-withdrawal-reinstatement framework.
18 PIF member state + 14 PIF dialogue partner ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive Pacific Ocean regional coverage framework.

1971 South Pacific Forum + 1985 Treaty of Rarotonga + 1989 Wellington Convention + 2000 Biketawa Declaration + 2000 renamed PIF + 2005 Agreement Establishing PIF + 2008 entry into force + 2018 Boe Declaration + 2021 Micronesian withdrawal + 2022 Suva Agreement + 2050 Strategy + 2024 Pacific Policing Initiative PIF-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

PIF Agreement + Biketawa Declaration + Boe Declaration + 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent + Treaty of Rarotonga + Wellington Convention formal sextet framework with formal Pacific-regional climate-displacement-children's-rights PA-intersection framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: Pacific Islands Forum jurisdiction sidecar — regional supranational Pacific-Ocean foundational-PIF meta-framework (PIF 1971/2000-renamed + Agreement Establishing PIF 2005/2008 + Biketawa Declaration 2000 + Boe Declaration 2018 + 2050 Strategy 2022 + Treaty of Rarotonga 1985 + Wellington Convention 1989 + Suva Agreement 2022 + Pacific Policing Initiative 2024 + Micronesian quintet 2021 withdrawal/2022 reinstatement). Only modern Pacific regional supranational framework operating on formal Pacific-Ocean framework globally + only modern regional supranational framework operating formal Australia-New-Zealand-Pacific-Islands developed-developing mixed-membership framework + only modern regional supranational framework granting formal Republics-monarchies-British-Crown-realms-mixed-membership framework + 165-million-square-kilometres geographic coverage spanning 30% Earth's surface + central jurisdiction of formal Boe Declaration climate-change-as-single-greatest-threat framework + central jurisdiction of formal 2050 Strategy for the Blue Pacific Continent + central jurisdiction of formal Micronesian-quintet-withdrawal-reinstatement framework + 18 PIF member states + 14 PIF dialogue partners + 1971 South Pacific Forum + 1985 Treaty of Rarotonga + 1989 Wellington Convention + 2000 Biketawa Declaration + renamed PIF + 2005 Agreement Establishing PIF + 2008 entry into force + 2018 Boe Declaration + 2021 Micronesian withdrawal + 2022 Suva Agreement + 2050 Strategy + 2024 Pacific Policing Initiative PIF-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: 2050 Strategy + Boe Declaration frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Pacific-regional cross-border-children's-rights framework + Pacific-regional climate-displacement-children's-rights framework.

Joins regional + supranational + Pacific-Ocean-foundational-PIF cluster + Pacific-Islands-Forum-only-Pacific-regional-supranational-framework-globally-distinctive + Australia-New-Zealand-Pacific-Islands-developed-developing-mixed-membership + Republics-monarchies-British-Crown-realms-mixed-membership + 165-million-square-kilometres-30-percent-Earth-surface + Boe-Declaration-2018-climate-change-as-single-greatest-threat + 2050-Strategy-Blue-Pacific-Continent + Micronesian-quintet-2021-withdrawal-2022-reinstatement-Suva-Agreement + 18-PIF-member-states + 14-PIF-dialogue-partners + Treaty-of-Rarotonga-1985-nuclear-free-zone + Wellington-Convention-1989-driftnet + Biketawa-Declaration-2000-crisis-response + 2024-Pacific-Policing-Initiative + Pacific-regional-climate-displacement-children-rights-PA-intersection clusters within the corpus.
